title: A New Book Project
article_text: I had just finished a rough draft of my book when my editor asked me to grab a coffee and discuss the next steps. She said she was entitled to see my work before anyone else, and I agreed with her involvement. My exposure to different cultures overseas helped me translate local stories into English. The establishment of a digital platform for writers was a dream I had long advocated for. I had to edit the formation of each chapter carefully, and I was happy to lend my notes to a friend. A pleasant surprise came when a dealer offered to seal a deal for my book's recovery rights. I had written the summary with a pen, but my assistant later typed it on a computer. The deposit for the book's printing was paid by the publisher, which was a burden I had not expected. If I had known about the conventional rules earlier, I would have avoided many mistakes. I wished I had taken more time to review the accurate details before the final seal was placed.

vocabulary.eng_def: to take something quickly
vocabulary.example: I grabbed my bag and ran to the bus.

vocabulary.eng_def: to give someone the right to do or have something
vocabulary.example: This ticket entitles you to a free drink.

vocabulary.eng_def: to give something to someone for a short time
vocabulary.example: Can you lend me your pen?

vocabulary.eng_def: the act of taking part in something
vocabulary.example: Her involvement in the project was very helpful.

vocabulary.eng_def: the experience of being in a situation

vocabulary.eng_def: following the usual or traditional way
vocabulary.example: They chose a conventional wedding.

vocabulary.eng_def: using or relating to computer technology
vocabulary.example: I prefer digital books over paper ones.

vocabulary.eng_def: to change words from one language to another

vocabulary.eng_def: to make changes to a text to improve it
vocabulary.example: He edited the article before sending it to the publisher.

vocabulary.eng_def: the way something is made or arranged
vocabulary.example: The formation of the group took several weeks.

vocabulary.eng_def: money paid as a first part of a payment
vocabulary.example: We paid a deposit on the new house.

vocabulary.eng_def: enjoyable or giving pleasure
vocabulary.example: The weather was very pleasant today.

vocabulary.eng_def: in or to a foreign country across the sea
vocabulary.example: She studied overseas for a year.

vocabulary.eng_def: to speak in support of an idea or plan
vocabulary.example: They advocate for better schools.

vocabulary.eng_def: the act of starting or creating something
vocabulary.example: The establishment of the company took two years.

vocabulary.eng_def: a short statement of the main points
vocabulary.example: Write a summary of the chapter.

vocabulary.eng_def: not smooth or not finished in detail
vocabulary.example: I made a rough drawing of the plan.

vocabulary.eng_def: a tool for writing with ink
vocabulary.example: She wrote the letter with a blue pen.

vocabulary.eng_def: the process of getting something back
vocabulary.example: The recovery of the lost data took a long time.

vocabulary.eng_def: to close or make something final; a mark that shows something is official
vocabulary.example: He sealed the envelope with tape.

What is the closest meaning of 'recovery'?
